channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: MedPlus Subsidiary Stores Face License Suspensions, ₹4.79 Lakh Revenue at Stake
item.description: MedPlus Health Services' subsidiary, Optival Health Solutions, has received three drug license suspension orders for stores in Karnataka, Telangana, and Andhra Pradesh. The temporary closures, lasting two to seven days, are expected to cause a combined revenue loss of about ₹4.79 lakh, highlighting ongoing regulatory compliance issues in pharmacy retail.

item.title: Aster DM Healthcare Loses ₹17.69 Cr in Strike; Court Halts Action
item.description: Aster DM Healthcare reported a revenue loss of ₹17.69 crore due to a nursing staff strike across its seven Kerala hospitals from March 9-13, 2026. The Kerala High Court intervened, ordering a deferral of the strike until March 19 and referring the wage dispute to mediation. The company implemented contingency measures, including mobilizing nurses from neighboring states, to ensure critical patient care continuity.

item.title: Suncity Synthetics Board to Discuss Share Sale to Raise Funds
item.description: Suncity Synthetics' board is set to meet on March 20, 2026, to consider selling shares and exploring other ways to raise money. The company faces financial difficulties and recently cut its capital structure, aiming to improve its financial health.

item.title: Suncity Synthetics Eyes Preferential Issue to Raise Funds March 20
item.description: Suncity Synthetics Limited is holding a board meeting on March 20, 2026, to discuss a preferential share issue and other fundraising avenues. The company, which reported a net loss of ₹56.44 lakh for the fiscal year ended March 2025, aims to secure needed capital amid financial challenges. It recently completed a capital reduction to address accumulated losses.

item.title: Bajaj Electricals to Fight ₹19.93 Crore GST Tax Demand
item.description: Bajaj Electricals is facing ₹19.93 crore in GST demands from Tamil Nadu for fiscal years 2019-20 and 2022-23. The company plans to explore legal challenges against the tax, interest, and penalties, noting that the financial impact is limited to the assessed amounts.

item.title: Sayaji Hotels Case With Ujaas Energy Settled, Legal Cloud Lifted
item.description: Sayaji Hotels (Indore) Limited has successfully resolved insolvency proceedings initiated by Ujaas Energy Limited. The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) closed the case on March 11, 2026, after the parties reached a settlement. This clears a significant legal and financial cloud for the hospitality firm, allowing it to focus on its operations and growth plans.

item.title: War Disrupts India's Medical Tourism; Sector Pivots to New Markets
item.description: Escalating Middle East conflict has drastically reduced international patient arrivals at Indian hospitals, with some reporting up to a 75% drop and expecting revenue impacts of 15-20% for March. The heavy reliance on patients from the Middle East, who often choose high-value procedures, has exposed a key weakness. To counter this, major hospital chains are speeding up plans to target new markets in Southeast Asia, Africa, and Central Asia, despite higher travel costs and logistical hurdles. Analysts predict a challenging quarter for the sector but remain optimistic long-term, noting the industry's past resilience and India's cost advantages.

item.title: Tax Tribunal: Adjacent Flats Can Be One Home for Capital Gains Tax Break
item.description: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) has ruled that multiple adjacent homes on the same floor can be considered a single 'house' for capital gains tax breaks. This decision allows taxpayers to combine nearby units for tax relief, focusing on physical connection and shared use, rather than treating each unit separately.

item.title: Fino Payments Bank Hits Record ₹2,900 Crore Deposits on Customer Trust Surge
item.description: Fino Payments Bank hit a record ₹2,900 crore in total deposits as of March 13, 2026. The balance jumped 9% in just two weeks, with about 1.5 lakh new accounts opened since late February. This growth signals increasing customer trust in its tech services and broad merchant network.

item.title: Zydus Lifesciences' Desidustat Drug Secures China Approval for CKD Anemia
item.description: Zydus Lifesciences has secured approval from China's National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) for its oral drug Desidustat, marketed as Oxemia™ in India, to treat anaemia in Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) patients. This significant market entry was facilitated by an exclusive licensing agreement with China Medical System Holdings (CMS), signed in 2020. The approval targets China's vast CKD patient population, estimated at over 120 million.

item.title: Meta Infotech Secures ₹1.91 Cr Banking Contract Renewal for IT Services
item.description: Meta Infotech Limited has renewed a ₹1.91 crore (excluding GST) contract with a major private sector bank for cloud workload and onsite IT resources. The contract extends through June 2027 for cloud services and February 2027 for onsite support. This deal highlights the company's ongoing partnership with its banking clients.

item.title: Silver Touch Technologies: Promoter Buys Shares, Lifts Stake to 21.11%
item.description: Silver Touch Technologies promoter Vipul Haridas Thakkar bought 4,400 shares on March 11-12, 2026, raising his stake from 20.38% to 21.11%. This comes as the company's stock faces volatility and recently hit a lower circuit.

item.title: Paisalo Digital's ₹1500cr NCDs Get Strong 'AA Stable' Credit Rating
item.description: Paisalo Digital Limited secured a 'BWR AA /Stable' credit rating for its proposed ₹1500 crore Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs). The rating, assigned by Brickwork Ratings India Private Limited for a long-term facility, indicates high safety for investors. This crucial rating will facilitate the company's fundraising efforts to support its lending activities.

item.title: India Stocks Plunge as Oil Hits $100, Mideast Tensions Rise
item.description: Indian markets suffered their sharpest weekly drop in four years, as the Nifty and Sensex fell sharply. Geopolitical tensions flared, pushing oil prices over $100 per barrel. Foreign investors sold heavily, but Indian institutions bought more to absorb the selling. Auto, banking, and metal sectors saw big losses, while energy held up. Investor confidence is shaky due to global events and inflation worries.

item.title: Elana Holdings Pledges 54% Asian Hotels North Stake for Financing
item.description: Elana Holdings Pte. Ltd. has pledged 54.37% of Asian Hotels (North) Limited's shares as collateral for financing. This move by the majority owner comes after the company's recent acquisition by Elana and follows previous defaults and financial struggles.

item.title: Maithan Alloys Buys ₹19.82 Crore Stake in Bank of India, No Control Intent
item.description: Maithan Alloys has acquired a 0.03% stake in Bank of India for ₹19.82 crore. The ferroalloy producer views this as a strategic investment for potential returns, explicitly stating no intention to seek management control. The move diversifies its financial assets.

item.title: WayCool Raises Rs 210 Crore from Lightrock India for Profit Push
item.description: Chennai-based agritech firm WayCool has raised Rs 210 crore ($22.7 million) in a rights issue, primarily backed by existing investor Lightrock India. This funding, the company's first major equity capital in nearly four years, comes after significant operational restructuring, including over 200 layoffs and leadership changes, as WayCool prioritizes achieving profitability.

item.title: India Pushes DME as LPG Alternative Amid Mideast Energy Fears
item.description: India is speeding up plans for dimethyl ether (DME) as a domestic alternative to LPG, driven by Middle East instability affecting global energy prices. The CSIR-National Chemical Laboratory is scaling up its DME pilot plant, developing a fuel compatible with current LPG systems. This move aims to improve energy security, cut import reliance, and could save around ₹9,500 crore annually by replacing 8% of LPG use.

item.title: Markolines Sees 16% Q3 Revenue Growth, Order Book Hits ₹695 Cr
item.description: Markolines Pavement Technologies posted a 16% increase in Q3 FY26 revenue and EBITDA, credited to strong operational execution. The company holds an unexecuted order book of ₹695 crore, strengthened by recent contract awards. Management projects FY26 revenue between ₹375-400 crore and aims for ₹1,000 crore in revenue within three to four years, reflecting an expansion into comprehensive highway maintenance and specialized services.

item.title: SEBI Chief: India Markets Resilient Amid Geopolitical Turmoil
item.description: SEBI Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey urged investors to stay patient amid global market volatility driven by geopolitical conflicts. He emphasized India's resilience through strong domestic fundamentals and a growing investor base, while noting SEBI's reforms to boost market efficiency, ease foreign investor access, and cut trading costs to shield markets from shocks and support growth.

item.title: PayU, CoRover.ai Unveil AI Voice Payments for 500 Million Indian Users
item.description: PayU and CoRover.ai have launched a strategic alliance to deploy multilingual, voice-first digital payments across India. By integrating PayU's payment gateway with CoRover's BharatGPT AI platform, the partnership aims to enable agentic transactions in over 100 Indian languages. This initiative targets India's expansive voice-first user base, with early deployments in niche sectors and plans for broader expansion into e-commerce, travel, and banking, leveraging emerging NPCI innovations like UPI Circle.

item.title: Manappuram Finance Q3 Profit ₹431 Cr, Faces ₹2.7 Lakh RBI Fine
item.description: Manappuram Finance reported ₹431 crore profit for Q3 FY25. Separately, the RBI fined the company ₹2.7 lakh for failing to defer variable pay for two Key Managerial Personnel. The company stated the penalty is small with no material impact and has revised its remuneration policy to comply.

item.title: Shemaroo Revises Shareholding to Show ESOP Dilution Impact
item.description: Shemaroo Entertainment has filed an updated shareholding pattern to account for potential dilution from its outstanding Employee Stock Options (ESOPs). This follows shareholder approval of a recent preferential issue and aims to boost transparency on the company's capital structure.

item.title: LPG Cut Forces HOCL Phenol Plant Shutdown, Declares Force Majeure
item.description: Hindustan Organic Chemicals (HOCL) has announced the temporary shutdown of its Phenol plant effective March 14, 2026. This critical step is due to BPCL discontinuing LPG supply, a vital feedstock, following a government directive. Declared a force majeure event, the duration and financial implications remain unestimatable. The company's Hydrogen Peroxide plant will continue unaffected.

item.title: India Stocks: Geopolitical Tensions Hit Oil, But Domestic Growth Shines
item.description: Indian markets faced volatility from West Asian tensions and soaring oil prices this week. Despite broad sector drops, analysts are watching select domestic companies showing resilience via infrastructure, renewable energy, and strong consumer demand, especially with heatwaves. However, investors should be wary of underlying risks and competition.

item.title: Eris Lifesciences Adds Ex-Banker Vineet Varma to Board
item.description: Eris Lifesciences Limited appointed Vineet Varma as a Non-executive, Independent Additional Director for a five-year term. His extensive wholesale banking experience is set to enhance the company's board oversight and governance.

item.title: JSW Steel Opens Mozambique Coal Mine; Green Steel Demand Risks Loom
item.description: JSW Steel has commenced operations at its Minas de Revuboè coking coal project in Mozambique, securing crucial raw material supply. The venture, however, is fraught with geopolitical instability and infrastructural challenges inherent to the region. Furthermore, the long-term demand for coking coal faces existential threats from the global push towards green steel technologies and stringent ESG mandates. JSW Steel's current market valuation also appears stretched compared to its peers, prompting a closer examination of the project's true strategic and financial implications.

item.title: Omnitech Engineering Reports ₹23 Cr Q3 Profit, 89% Income Surge Post-IPO
item.description: Omnitech Engineering achieved strong Q3 FY26 results, reporting standalone income up 89% to ₹142.37 crore and a profit of ₹23.05 crore. This performance follows its March 2026 IPO, which raised ₹391.74 crore to support expansion and cut debt.

item.title: India's Tankers Pass Hormuz, Easing Energy Fears Amid Rising Risks
item.description: Indian tankers Shivalik and Nanda Devi have successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz after diplomatic talks with Iran. This passage eases immediate concerns for India's vital energy imports, which heavily depend on the route. However, the transit occurs amidst high geopolitical tensions, soaring insurance costs, and elevated oil prices, underscoring India's delicate energy security balance. The Shipping Corporation of India faces operational tests from regional instability and internal regulatory issues.

item.title: Dilip Buildcon Wins ₹160 Crore Odisha Road Project
item.description: Dilip Buildcon has been named the L-1 bidder for a ₹160.20 crore road diversion project in Odisha. The Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) project has an 18-month completion timeline and adds significantly to the company's order book.

item.title: Mahip Industries: Director's Gift Puts His Stake Below 50%
item.description: Mahip Industries Limited, a manufacturer of corrugated boxes, announced a significant share transfer within its promoter group. Whole-Time Director Rajiv Govindram Agarwal gifted 9,50,000 equity shares, representing 4.94% of the company's voting capital, to Mamta Rajiv Agrawal via an off-market transaction on March 12, 2026. This reduces Agarwal's direct shareholding from 52.52% to 47.58%. The move is an inter-promoter transfer, aiming to restructure ownership within the group.

item.title: Mahip Industries: Agarwal Ups Stake to 6.39% Amidst CIRP, Losses
item.description: Mrs. Mamta Rajivkumar Agarwal acquired 950,000 shares in Mahip Industries Limited on March 12, 2026, raising her stake to 6.39%. The company is currently in Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP), reporting large losses and a qualified audit opinion over compliance issues.

item.title: Godrej Properties: Promoter Confidence High as Stake Holds Firm Above 51%
item.description: Godrej Properties' promoter group, Anamudi Real Estates LLP, acquired 70 shares on March 12, 2026. The minor purchase maintains their substantial stake of around 51.66%, reflecting strong promoter confidence in the real estate company.

item.title: Godawari Power & Ispat Shareholders Approve Logistics Sector Entry
item.description: Godawari Power & Ispat Limited shareholders have approved a special resolution to amend the company's Memorandum of Association. The decision, made at an Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) on March 14, 2026, formally allows the company to enter the logistics sector. This is a significant strategic diversification for the integrated steel and power producer, creating a new growth avenue.

item.title: Euro Multivision Appoints Secretary, Auditors to Finalize Liquidation Compliance
item.description: Euro Multivision Limited's board has approved key appointments to bolster its compliance framework. Ms. Ankita Mohta will serve as Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, effective December 15, 2025. M/s. MGS Reddy & Co. have been appointed as Statutory Auditors for a five-year term starting FY 2023-24. These roles are crucial for finalizing pending compliance requirements following the company's acquisition under liquidation and preparing for its transition as a going concern.

item.title: SBI Warns: El Nino, Geopolitics to Boost India Inflation in 2026
item.description: A State Bank of India report warns that potential El Nino weather and ongoing geopolitical conflicts could significantly increase global prices in 2026. This poses a major threat to India's agricultural and energy sectors. While some expect lower commodity prices from surplus supply, climate instability and conflicts introduce considerable upside risks, with developing economies like India likely to bear the heaviest economic burden.

item.title: Hindalco Pauses Aluminium Sales Amid Gas Crisis, Prices Surge
item.description: Hindalco Industries has halted sales of extruded aluminium products, citing gas supply disruptions linked to the Middle East conflict and shipping route issues. This spotlights vulnerabilities in India's gas-dependent manufacturing sector. The company declared force majeure, affecting automotive and construction, as global aluminium prices soar to four-year highs.

item.title: India LPG Crisis: Delivery Platforms Strain, QSRs Stay Strong
item.description: India's commercial LPG shortage is challenging the food service industry. Organized QSR chains, such as Jubilant FoodWorks and Westlife Foodworld, are mostly keeping operations running thanks to stored supplies and kitchens that use electricity. However, food delivery platforms, particularly Zomato, face significant pressure as standalone restaurants struggle with disruptions, potentially leading to fewer orders and affecting the platforms' broader operations.

item.title: Atul Auto Names Auto Veteran Dr. K.C. Vora Independent Director
item.description: Atul Auto Limited has appointed Dr. K.C. Vora as an Independent Director, effective March 15, 2026, for a three-year term, pending shareholder approval. With over 40 years in the auto industry and academia, Dr. Vora is set to enhance the company's governance and strategic direction, offering valuable insights for the changing automotive sector.

item.title: Bridge Securities: Harshad Panchal Sells Millions of Shares, Stake Falls to 12.45%
item.description: Harshad Amrutlal Panchal, a significant shareholder in Bridge Securities Limited, has sold 8,10,463 equity shares. The transactions occurred in two phases between September 2025 and March 2026, lowering his stake from 16.81% to 12.45% of the company's voting capital. The filings were made on March 13, 2026.
